# commerce-changeset llms.txt

## Stack
Next.js 16.2.1 (App Router), React 19, TypeScript (strict mode)
Tailwind CSS v4 (CSS-based config in globals.css, no JS config file)
Base UI + shadcn/ui + CVA for components
Auth0 v4 (@auth0/nextjs-auth0) for authentication
Vercel AI SDK (ai ^6.0.134) for model integration
@auth0/ai-vercel for Auth0-aware AI interactions (Token Vault, CIBA)
zod for schema validation
json-rules-engine for business rule evaluation

## Models
Vercel AI SDK provides the model integration layer.
@auth0/ai-vercel connects Auth0 sessions to AI interactions.
An Anthropic API key is configured for Claude model access.

## Agent Architecture
Four-agent system with OAuth 2.0 On-Behalf-Of (OBO) delegation:

Orchestrator Agent (lib/agents/orchestrator.ts)
- Receives natural-language request, gathers current state via Reader Agent
- LLM decomposes prompt into discrete operations with diffs
- Policy engine classifies each operation's risk tier
- Assembles change set; delegates execution to Writer Agent post-approval

Reader Agent (lib/agents/reader.ts)
- LLM-driven agent with 4 Google Sheets tools via Token Vault OBO
- Tools: get_products, get_pricing, get_launch_schedule, get_launch_windows
- Used for initial data gathering and post-execution verification read-back

Writer Agent (lib/agents/writer.ts)
- Deterministic (not LLM-driven); loops over approved operations
- Tools: update_price (column E), set_promo_status (column F), update_inventory_flag (column G), bulk_price_change (column E, batch), create_product (append row)
- Only activated after change set approval via CIBA

Notifier Agent (lib/agents/notifier.ts)
- Deterministic; builds HTML email from change set and receipt
- Tools: send_launch_notification, send_execution_receipt via Gmail API + Token Vault OBO
- Non-blocking in executor pipeline

## Policy Engine
json-rules-engine evaluates each operation against 4 risk tiers:
- Tier 0 READ: auto-approve (no user interaction)
- Tier 1 NOTIFY: auto-approve with notification
- Tier 2 WRITE: requires CIBA approval via Guardian push notification
- Tier 3 BULK: escalated CIBA (price change >25% or affectedRecords >1)
Default-deny fallback for unmatched operations.
PolicyDecision includes: decision, tier, reason, ruleName, scopeRequested.

## Change Set Lifecycle
Status state machine: draft -> pending_approval -> approved -> executing -> completed
Additional statuses: partial_failure, expired, rolled_back
Each operation carries: id, agent, action, target, tier, policyExplanation, diff, rollback.
RiskSummary aggregates: maxTier, operationsByTier, requiresCIBA, autonomousOps, approvalRequiredOps.

## CIBA Approval Flow
CIBA via Auth0 Guardian push notifications (lib/changeset/approval.ts).
Dynamic binding message built from change set content (64-char limit).
Block mode: polls internally, resolves on approve/deny/expire.
Skipped when requiresCIBA is false or 0 writer operations.

## Execution Pipeline (lib/changeset/executor.ts)
1. CIBA approval step (if required)
2. Writer Agent executes approved write operations
3. Verification read-back via Reader Agent (expected vs actual)
4. Execution receipt: OBO chain, agent delegations, verification, audit hash (SHA-256)
5. Notifier Agent dispatch (non-blocking)
6. Final status: completed | partial_failure
7. Rollback: reversal changesets built via rollback-builder.ts, linked by rollbackOf field

## Endpoints
/auth/login        — Initiate Auth0 login (handled by middleware)
/auth/logout       — Initiate Auth0 logout (handled by middleware)
/auth/callback     — Auth0 callback after authentication (handled by middleware)
/auth/connect      — Connected Accounts endpoint (enabled via SDK)
/api/auth/[auth0]  — Auth0 route handler (GET)
/api/spike/token-vault    — Auth0 Token Vault -> Google Sheets integration (GET, env-gated)
/api/spike/connect-google — Google Connected Accounts linking flow (GET, env-gated)
/api/spike/ciba           — CIBA + Guardian push notification approval (GET, env-gated)
/api/reader               — Reader Agent: natural-language product/pricing/launch queries (POST, authenticated)
/api/orchestrator         — Orchestrator Agent: decomposes prompts into change sets with diffs and policy decisions (POST, authenticated)
/api/orchestrator/execute — Execute approved change set: CIBA approval, write execution, verification, notification, receipt (POST, authenticated)
/api/orchestrator/rollback — Build reversal changeset from executed original (POST, authenticated)
/dashboard                — Chat dashboard for interacting with agents and viewing change sets (authenticated)

## UI Components
Chat interface (app/dashboard/chat.tsx): state machine (idle/loading/draft/executing/complete/error), suggested prompts, inline tool call indicators, execute button for CIBA changesets.
ChangeSet view (components/changeset/changeset-view.tsx): composite header, risk summary, operations, rollback, receipt.
Operation card (components/changeset/operation-card.tsx): agent badge, action, target, clickable risk badge, diff, result.
Risk badge (components/changeset/risk-badge.tsx): click to expand full policy explanation (decision, rule, reason, scope).
Agent badge (components/changeset/agent-badge.tsx): color-coded by agent (Reader=blue, Writer=amber, Notifier=green).
Risk summary (components/changeset/risk-summary.tsx): aggregate risk, CIBA badge, autonomy indicators.
Diff view (components/changeset/diff-view.tsx): two-column before/after per field.
Execution receipt (components/changeset/execution-receipt.tsx): OBO chain, agent delegations with status indicators, context boundary indicators, verification, autonomy breakdown, audit hash.
Rollback section (components/changeset/rollback-section.tsx): collapsible rollback instructions per operation.

## Google Workspace Integration
Google Sheets: Products sheet (SKU, Name, Category, Base Price, Promo Price, Promo Active, Inventory)
Google Sheets: Launch Schedule sheet
Read/write via Auth0 Token Vault OBO (RFC 8693 token exchange)
Gmail: send notifications via Token Vault OBO

## Agent Instructions
AGENTS.md       — Comprehensive coding conventions, architecture, and examples
CONTRIBUTING.md — Branching, PR process, coding standards, review checklist
CLAUDE.md       — References @AGENTS.md for Claude Code agent behavior

## Prompt Conventions
Use zod schemas for all AI input/output validation.
Use json-rules-engine for business rule evaluation.
Validate AI-generated outputs with zod before use.
Server-side only for Auth0 session access (never in client components).
